atmosphere: Textured waste-terminal material with clear instrument signals.

oldWarningYellow.description: Control and tag pressure should read as old-warning yellow, not mud gold or Ember orange.

terminalLichenGreen.description: Callable structure should read as terminal lichen green without becoming generic neon.

sagePropertyStructure.description: Object fields and member structure should carry a quieter sage green presence so Moss reads as structured growth, not neutral parchment.

oxidizedMethodBridge.description: Methods should bridge callable green and oxidized blue so high-frequency calls add rhythm without collapsing into keyword yellow or type blue.

oxidizedCrtBlue.description: Types and numbers should hold the cool structural lane.

lacqueredPaperString.description: Strings should feel like faded lacquered paper or reed-straw, separate from keyword yellow and method green.

oxidizedStructuralPunctuation.description: Punctuation should be a low-weight dry oxide accent that adds light-theme rhythm without becoming a fourth headline lane.

antiGoals: blue becoming a second full foreground
materialRules: Keep charcoal, parchment, and oxidized metal stable while adjusting syntax clarity.
Age belongs mainly to surface and material; syntax lanes must stay crisp.
Use punctuation as a low-weight structural accent, not as a dominant semantic color.
